authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.24 - Condition of participation: Medical record services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal hospital regulation for medical record service organization, prompt completion, authentication, retention, and record content. It directly constrains hospital HIM queue planning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: hospital medical record service governance, completion, authentication, retention, and record-content prerequisite handling

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 482.43 - Condition of participation: Discharge planning
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal hospital discharge planning rule that makes timely transfer of necessary medical information a hospital workflow requirement. It supports care-transition record availability and escalation scenarios.

authoritative_sources.title: Uses and Disclosures for Treatment, Payment, and Health Care Operations
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. This guidance defines treatment, payment, healthcare operations, role-based access, and minimum-necessary limits relevant to HIM queue packets.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI handling for treatment, payment, and healthcare operations

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR guidance is authoritative for limiting PHI uses, disclosures, and requests to the necessary scope. It directly constrains PHI-bearing HIM status summaries and external request packets.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 164.508 - Uses and disclosures for which an authorization is required
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Binding HIPAA authorization rule for disclosures requiring authorization. It gates ROI, external request, and payer packet work when authorization authority is missing.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 164.526 - Amendment of protected health information
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Binding HIPAA amendment rule for accepting, denying, appending, linking, and documenting amendment requests. It prevents HIM coordination from turning into record rewriting.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: amendment queue routing and record integrity boundary

authoritative_sources.title: Complying With Medical Record Documentation Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS MLN guidance for documentation sufficiency, CERT review, insufficient documentation errors, missing forms, missing signatures, orders, and records needed to support payment review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: documentation sufficiency, coding-support, billing-support, audit-support, and payer-review packet readiness

authoritative_sources.title: Complying With Medicare Signature Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS guidance for signature, attestation, signature log, and authentication evidence. It shapes unsigned-record deficiency and coding-support prioritization.

authoritative_sources.title: Medical Record Maintenance & Access Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS provider guidance for maintaining, retaining, furnishing, and granting access to medical records. It supports retrieval, audit readiness, and retention-aware HIM planning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: record maintenance, retention, retrieval, furnishing, and access readiness

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administers Medicare and publishes this manual as official public guidance for claim processing, payment, remittance, correction, and reopening workflows. It constrains HIM claim-support and denial-support handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: charge, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, and claim-support dependency handling

authoritative_sources.title: Fundamentals of the Legal Health Record and Designated Record Set
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHIMA is the recognized HIM professional body. The practice brief provides vendor-neutral guidance for legal health record, designated record set, source-system matrices, retention, disclosure readiness, and external-record ambiguity.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: HIM record-set definition, source-system mapping, disclosure readiness, retention, and external-record handling

authoritative_sources.title: Workflow Assessment for Health IT Toolkit
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is a federal healthcare quality and health IT workflow authority. The toolkit supports workflow mapping, redesign, bottleneck identification, and handoff planning for health IT-enabled work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: workflow mapping, constraint identification, capacity review, and handoff redesign

authoritative_sources.title: CAQH CORE Operating Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAQH CORE operating rules are recognized national healthcare administrative workflow rules for eligibility, claim status, payment, remittance, prior authorization, and attachment transactions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, prior authorization, claim status, remittance, and attachment transaction awareness

authoritative_sources.title: HL7 FHIR R4 ServiceRequest
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HL7 is the recognized health interoperability standards body. FHIR ServiceRequest grounds vendor-neutral terminology for orders, referrals, transfer requests, and diagnostic or therapeutic service requests.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: EHR request, order, referral, and service dependency normalization

workflow_stages.name: HIM demand intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Classify queue demand by record completion, ROI, amendment, documentation support, coding-support, claim-support, denial-support, payer-audit, care-transition, or out-of-scope authority request.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Patient, member, encounter, payer, and source truth validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate identifiers, service dates, facility, service setting, payer, plan, legal health record or designated record set scope, source-system hierarchy, authorization, referral, eligibility, and privacy constraints before sequencing work.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Documentation, authorization, and evidence prerequisite review
workflow_stages.description: Check orders, encounter documentation, signatures, attestations, consents, authorizations, referrals, eligibility responses, discharge documentation, charge capture, coding-support records, claim-support artifacts, remittance, denial, and appeal records for administrative readiness.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess backlog, approved service levels, staffing capacity, owner availability, system access, queue aging, payer deadlines, care-transition timing, privacy constraints, and downstream patient, member, revenue, or compliance impact.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Recommendation-only schedule, priority, and allocation support
workflow_stages.description: Recommend queue order, staffing allocation, sequencing, deferral, refresh cadence, or next-owner package using approved rules without claiming final approval authority.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, privacy, clinical, payer, coding, and approval escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when missing records, PHI controls, clinical risk, payer-policy interpretation, coding decision, legal/privacy decision, source conflict, or final approval would change the answer.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: Eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, coverage, payer-plan, and service-date records
Encounter documentation, orders, discharge summaries, care-transition packets, clinical-administrative notes, consents, signatures, attestations, and deficiency queue items
Legal health record and designated record set definitions, source-system matrices, retention matrices, disclosure logs, ROI requests, amendment requests, and external request logs
Charge capture records, coding queries, claim records, remittance advice, CARC/RARC detail, denial records, appeal windows, payer audit requests, and attachment manifests
HIM queue dashboards, backlog reports, aging reports, SLA targets, staffing rosters, productivity baselines, priority matrices, approval matrices, and escalation logs
Privacy restrictions, consent and proxy authority records, role-based access controls, audit logs, legal hold notes, and minimum-necessary handling constraints
Operational status summaries, constraint logs, schedule or allocation recommendations, approval-needed summaries, and adjacent-specialist routing notes
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation systems
Health information management workflow and chart-completion systems
Document management, imaging, scanning, and external-record repositories
Release-of-information, amendment, disclosure log, and privacy workflow systems
Practice management, ADT, registration, scheduling, and patient access systems
Eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, utilization review, case management, and payer portal systems
Coding, charge capture, revenue cycle, claim, clearinghouse, remittance, denial, appeal, and AR work queues
CRM, patient/member access, communication, and patient portal systems
Audit, compliance, legal hold, source-control, role-based access, and approval systems
Tenant policy repositories for record retention, source precedence, priority rules, approval rules, and privacy constraints

allowed: Classify HIM planning work as in scope, out of scope, blocked, or requiring orchestrator return.
Validate whether required tenant facts, patient/member records, source systems, authorization/referral/eligibility dependencies, documentation artifacts, and privacy constraints are present.
Assess queue demand, backlog, approved service levels, staffing envelope, source conflicts, access blockers, and downstream patient/member, revenue, or compliance risk.
Recommend queue sequence, allocation, refresh cadence, deferral, and next-owner routing using approved priority rules.
Prepare schedule or allocation recommendations, constraint logs, approval-needed summaries, operational status summaries, and escalation notes with traceable evidence.
escalate: Required tenant facts, jurisdiction, payer, plan, facility, service setting, source precedence, patient/member status, service dates, authorization, referral, eligibility, privacy, or approval context is missing and would change the recommendation.
EHR, HIM, ROI, document, practice management, revenue cycle, payer, utilization, case management, patient access, audit, or compliance systems materially conflict.
Public guidance, tenant policy, payer contract, state Medicaid rule, facility policy, privacy rule, legal instruction, or accreditation expectation materially conflict.
PHI handling, minimum-necessary scope, requester authority, personal representative status, legal hold, or privacy obligation changes the answer.

Clinical safety, diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, coding, payer-policy interpretation, claim adjudication, denial appeal, financial approval, staffing approval, legal review, privacy approval, or final operational approval is required.
refuse: Requests to provide final budget, staffing, overtime, release, amendment, coding, payer, claim, denial, medical necessity, clinical, legal, privacy, or operational approval.
Requests to author, rewrite, infer, backdate, delete, conceal, or clinically interpret record content.
Requests to bypass missing authorization, referral, eligibility, documentation, consent, minimum-necessary, or source-of-truth evidence.
Requests to disclose PHI without validated authority, approved access, and minimum-necessary constraints.
Requests to treat generic project management, scheduling, or document processing as sufficient for hospital HIM without hospital, payer, record, privacy, and source-system evidence.

domain_failure_modes: The lane behaves like generic records administration and ignores legal health record, designated record set, HIPAA, hospital Conditions of Participation, source-system, and payer documentation constraints.
Queue order is recommended from backlog volume alone without authorization, referral, eligibility, discharge, payer deadline, clinical risk, privacy, or compliance dependencies.
The lane certifies documentation completeness despite conflicting EHR, HIM, document management, and audit sources.
The lane exposes PHI in broad exports or status summaries without role-based access, requester authority, and minimum-necessary handling.
The lane chooses codes, evaluates medical necessity, interprets payer policy, or decides claim/appeal outcomes from HIM evidence.
The lane presents staffing, overtime, budget, release, amendment, or operational recommendations as final approvals.
The lane ignores tenant-specific facility, payer, service-setting, state Medicaid, source-precedence, priority, and approval rules.
The lane misses patient/member harm from bad routing, incomplete care-transition records, or failure to escalate clinical safety questions.
The lane advances claim-support or denial-support work when eligibility, referral, authorization, order, signature, or documentation prerequisites are absent.
The lane resolves conflicting public, payer, tenant, privacy, or jurisdictional sources by guessing instead of escalating.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: Tenant policy, facility/service-setting context, source-system hierarchy, patient/member identifiers, service dates, payer/plan, eligibility, referral, authorization, orders, signatures, attestations, documentation completeness, requester authority, consent/proxy constraints, privacy restrictions, role-based access, minimum-necessary handling, and approved priority and approval rules gate safe HIM planning.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: The truth set is established by the tenant-approved source hierarchy across EHR/EMR, HIM workflow, legal health record/designated record set policy, ADT/registration, practice management, eligibility/referral/authorization systems, payer portals, utilization/case management, coding/charge/claim/remittance/denial systems, ROI/disclosure logs, document repositories, and audit/compliance systems.

required_workflow_question_answers.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
required_workflow_question_answers.answer: The lane stops at administrative readiness, prioritization, evidence packaging, and routing. Licensed clinical authority begins at diagnosis, treatment, patient safety, medical necessity, level-of-care, or clinical urgency judgment. Privacy authority begins at legal/privacy interpretation, requester authority disputes, disclosure approval, and minimum-necessary exceptions. Payer authority begins at coverage, authorization, medical policy, claim adjudication, denial, appeal, and reimbursement interpretation.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant legal health record versus designated record set definitions
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant LHR/DRS policy and source-system matrix before making record-set-specific recommendations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State Medicaid, state medical-record, and facility retention variations
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Escalate or apply tenant-supplied jurisdictional rules when they alter retention, release, amendment, or documentation timing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payer-specific authorization, referral, attachment, and appeal rules
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require payer/plan-specific sources and route payer-policy interpretation to payer operations or revenue cycle owners.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: External records in legal health record
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: AHIMA notes organizational and legal-policy variation; require tenant policy and legal owner when external-record classification changes disclosure or record completeness.

market_ready_research_conclusion.summary: The source set covers healthcare regulator boundaries, payer/provider operating evidence, HIM terminology, health IT workflow planning, and transaction/source-system handling. Remaining ambiguities are tenant or jurisdiction runtime facts and are handled by explicit refusal, escalation, or orchestrator-return rules.
